movie.title: They Fight
movie.overview: Reformed ex-convict Walt Manigan returns home to rebuild his family with the love of his life, Ketta. His path leads him to a boxing gym inside a D.C. youth recreation center about to be closed. There, he works under the wise coach Slim to mentor a group of boys to train for the junior national boxing tournament, all leading to a showdown between best friends Quincey and Peanut as they battle for a spot on the U.S. Junior Olympic Team.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: A reformed ex-convict mentors at-risk youth at a struggling D.C. boxing gym and guides two best friends toward a Junior Olympic showdown.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: Walt Manigan, a reformed ex-convict, returns home to rebuild his life with his partner Ketta and finds purpose at a D.C. youth boxing gym facing closure. Working under veteran coach Slim, he mentors a group of boys preparing for the junior national boxing tournament. The story builds to a tense rivalry between close friends Quincey and Peanut as they compete for a spot on the U.S. Junior Olympic Team. Along the way, Walt finds redemption and the gym's community fights to stay alive.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: Walt Manigan is a reformed ex-convict who returns to his hometown hoping to rebuild his family with the love of his life, Ketta. Struggling to find direction, he discovers a boxing gym located inside a Washington D.C. youth recreation center that is on the verge of being shut down. At the gym, Walt meets Slim, a wise and experienced coach who takes him under his wing and helps him channel his energy into mentoring local boys. Walt begins training a group of young athletes for the upcoming junior national boxing tournament, using discipline and care to keep them focused. As the boys progress, two of them—best friends Quincey and Peanut—emerge as the gym's top talents and closest competitors. The threat of the recreation center's closure looms over the team, adding urgency to their training and unity. Walt grows as a mentor and role model, confronting his past while helping the kids envision a better future. Tensions rise as Quincey and Peanut must eventually face each other in the ring to earn a place on the U.S. Junior Olympic Team. Their friendship is tested by ambition, loyalty, and the weight of representing their community. In the end, the gym's fight for survival and the boys' journey reflect themes of redemption, brotherhood, and hope.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: They Fight follows Walt Manigan, a reformed ex-convict who returns home to restart his life with his partner Ketta. He finds unexpected purpose at a D.C. youth recreation center boxing gym that is scheduled to close, where he works alongside seasoned coach Slim to mentor a group of young boxers. As Walt helps the boys train for the junior national tournament, the story centers on the bond between two best friends, Quincey and Peanut, who must eventually compete against each other for a coveted spot on the U.S. Junior Olympic Team. Blending drama and action, the film explores redemption, community, and the power of mentorship in the face of adversity.

story_structure.climax: Best friends Quincey and Peanut face off in a decisive match to earn a spot on the U.S. Junior Olympic Team, testing loyalty and ambition.
story_structure.conflict: Walt discovers a boxing gym in a D.C. youth recreation center facing imminent closure and must help mentor at-risk boys while confronting personal and external obstacles.
story_structure.resolution: The outcome of the showdown and the gym's fate are revealed, showing the impact of Walt's mentorship and the boys' growth.
story_structure.introduction: Walt Manigan, a reformed ex-convict, returns home to rebuild his life and family with Ketta, seeking redemption and stability.
story_structure.risingAction: Under Coach Slim's guidance, Walt trains a group of boys for the junior national boxing tournament, building teamwork and hope as the gym's future hangs in the balance.
